summary: CLOB Fill WebSocket Feature Testing Complete. All 29 backend API tests passed. Backend correctly implements fill_ws graceful degradation without credentials: GET /api/health/fill-ws returns proper health object with connected=false, has_credentials=false, no errors. GET /api/status includes fill_ws_connected, fill_ws_has_credentials, fill_ws_health object, and fill_update_method='polling'. GET /api/execution/status includes live_adapter.fill_ws_health and fill_update_method. GET /api/execution/mode returns valid credentials info. All existing endpoints work (no regressions). Frontend displays 'Fill Updates: polling' in System Status section. However, 'Fill WS' badge is NOT displayed because frontend relies solely on WebSocket data (which has connectivity issues in preview environment). Backend REST API returns all fill_ws data correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Fill WS badge (lines 117-123) not displayed because health.fill_ws_health is only populated via WebSocket, not REST API fallback
ui_bugs.root_cause: Frontend relies on WebSocket snapshot for stats.health.fill_ws_health. When WebSocket connection fails (common in cloud proxy environments), the badge never renders.
ui_bugs.suggested_fix: Add REST API fallback in useApi.js to fetch /api/status and populate stats.health, or fetch fill_ws health specifically from /api/health/fill-ws

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket connection to wss://...preview.emergentagent.com/api/ws fails intermittently ('WebSocket is closed before the connection is established')
affected_selectors: System Status section - Fill WS badge
integration_issues.note: This is a preview environment/cloud proxy issue, not a code bug. WebSocket works correctly when connection succeeds.

backend_api_tests: GET /api/health/fill-ws has all required fields (connected, has_credentials, connect_count, disconnect_count, messages_received, trade_events, confirmed_fills, failed_fills, subscribed_markets, last_message_seconds_ago, last_error)
fill-ws connected is boolean (false without credentials)
fill-ws has_credentials is boolean (false without credentials)
fill-ws no credentials causes connected=false, has_credentials=false (graceful degradation)
fill-ws metrics initialized to valid integers (all >= 0)
fill-ws no error when gracefully degraded (last_error is null)
stats.health.fill_ws_health object exists with nested fields
fill_update_method is 'polling' when WS not connected
Fill WS gracefully degraded - no errors, not connected
Status endpoint unaffected by missing credentials

action_items: MEDIUM: Add REST API fallback for Fill WS health data in frontend. Currently Overview.jsx line 117 conditionally renders Fill WS badge only when health.fill_ws_health is truthy, but this data only comes via WebSocket. Add a fetch to /api/status or /api/health/fill-ws in useApi.js to ensure the badge displays even when WebSocket connection fails.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend Overview.jsx relies solely on WebSocket for stats.health.fill_ws_health data. Should add REST fallback to ensure Fill WS badge always displays.
Backend implementation is correct - all endpoints return proper fill_ws data with graceful degradation when credentials missing.

success_rate.frontend: 91% (Fill WS badge not displaying due to WebSocket connectivity, but all pages load and 'Fill Updates: polling' displays correctly)

test_credentials: None required - testing graceful degradation without Polymarket API credentials
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fill WS Feature (Phase 8C): Backend fully implemented and tested. ClobFillWsClient at /app/backend/engine/clob_fill_ws.py gracefully handles missing credentials (connected=false, has_credentials=false, no errors). LiveAdapter at /app/backend/engine/live_adapter.py has set_fill_ws, on_ws_fill, and status property with fill_ws_health. Backend endpoints: (1) GET /api/health/fill-ws returns full health object (2) GET /api/status includes stats.health.fill_ws_connected, fill_ws_has_credentials, fill_ws_health object, fill_update_method (3) GET /api/execution/status includes live_adapter.fill_ws_health and fill_update_method. Frontend issue: Fill WS badge at Overview.jsx lines 117-123 doesn't render because it relies on WebSocket data which has connectivity issues in preview environment. Fix needed: Add REST API fallback to populate stats.health in the Zustand store.

features_tested.fill_ws_health_endpoint: PASS - Returns proper health object with all required fields
features_tested.status_endpoint_fill_ws_integration: PASS - fill_ws_connected, fill_ws_has_credentials, fill_ws_health, fill_update_method all present
features_tested.execution_status_fill_ws_integration: PASS - live_adapter has fill_ws_health and fill_update_method
features_tested.fill_update_method_polling_fallback: PASS - Shows 'polling' when WS not connected
features_tested.frontend_fill_updates_display: PASS - 'Fill Updates: polling' shown in System Status
features_tested.frontend_fill_ws_badge: FAIL - Not displayed due to WebSocket data dependency
